reasons: Diggie reduces Luo Yi's group-control payoff when she lands a multi-target reaction.
Luo Yi is strongest when allies are pulled or displaced together; Diggie makes that burst-control window less reliable.

proof.summary: Luo Yi wants grouped enemies to suffer Yin-Yang reaction control, while Diggie can protect nearby allies during the key CC window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Luo Yi's real combo.
Allies avoid stacking before Diggie is ready.
failureCases: Luo Yi baits Diggie's ultimate with poke.
Luo Yi's team uses Diversion to attack from an unexpected angle.

reasons: Kagura can remove debuffs and dash, making it harder for Luo Yi to complete the reaction setup.
Kagura also threatens Luo Yi from mage range without needing to stand grouped with teammates.

proof.summary: Luo Yi wants to mark and pull enemies through Yin-Yang reactions, while Kagura can remove debuffs, dash away, and reposition with her umbrella.
worksBestWhen: Kagura saves Rasho Umbrella Flee for the real mark/control threat.
Kagura keeps enough distance to avoid being grouped with allies.
failureCases: Luo Yi catches Kagura without umbrella positioning.
Luo Yi's team teleports behind Kagura with Diversion.

reasons: Luo Yi is vulnerable to ambush because she depends on positioning and setup time.
Natalia can silence and burst Luo Yi before she freely cycles marks.

proof.summary: Luo Yi wants to stand at safe mage range and set up reactions, while Natalia can threaten her from camouflage and break her casting rhythm with silence.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks before Luo Yi's team groups around her.
Natalia tracks Luo Yi's rotation paths and bush control.
failureCases: Luo Yi stays behind tank vision.
Luo Yi's teammates collapse on Natalia after reveal.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge or bypass Luo Yi's skill zones and directly threaten her backline position.
Luo Yi is punishable after she uses skill charges or misses the reaction setup.

proof.summary: Luo Yi wants enemies to be predictable and grouped, while Lancelot can dash through angles and use invulnerability timing to punish her cooldown windows.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot enters after Luo Yi spends Dispersion charges.
Luo Yi is separated from hard peel.
failureCases: Lancelot dashes into grouped reaction zones.
Luo Yi's team layers CC after his invulnerability ends.

reasons: Yve can contest Luo Yi from long range and force spacing discipline through zoning.
Her slows and battlefield control reduce Luo Yi's ability to freely walk into reaction range.

proof.summary: Luo Yi wants enemies close enough for repeated mark reactions, while Yve can zone from range and slow approach paths.
worksBestWhen: Yve positions outside Luo Yi's reaction range.
The team spreads instead of stacking inside Luo Yi's combo area.
failureCases: Luo Yi's team uses Diversion to flank Yve.
Yve is caught while channeling or overextended.
Luo Yi lands marks on grouped allies before Yve controls space.
